chapter_title: Oil seeds and oleaginous fruits; miscellaneous grains, seeds and fruit; industrial or medicinal plants; straw and fodder
inclusions: Oil seeds and oleaginous fruits (soya beans, ground-nuts, copra, linseed, rape/colza seeds, sunflower seeds, palm nuts and kernels, cotton seeds, castor oil seeds, sesamum seeds, mustard seeds, safflower seeds, melon seeds, poppy seeds, etc.)
Flours and meals of oil seeds or oleaginous fruits, other than those of mustard (heading 12.08)
Seeds, fruit and spores of a kind used for sowing (heading 12.09), including beet seeds, grass seeds, seeds of ornamental flowers, vegetable seeds, seeds of forest trees, seeds of fruit trees
Hop cones, fresh or dried, whether or not ground, powdered or in pellets; lupulin (heading 12.10)

Plants and parts of plants used primarily in perfumery, pharmacy or for insecticidal/fungicidal purposes including ginseng roots, coca leaf, poppy straw, ephedra, bark of African cherry, cannabis, liquorice roots, pyrethrum, sandalwood chips, agarwood chips (heading 12.11)
Locust beans, seaweeds and other algae, sugar beet and sugar cane, chicory roots, fruit stones and kernels, other vegetable products for human consumption NES (heading 12.12)
Cereal straw and husks, unprepared, whether or not chopped, ground, pressed or in pellets (heading 12.13)
Swedes, mangolds, fodder roots, hay, lucerne, clover, sainfoin, forage kale, lupines, vetches and similar forage products (heading 12.14)

exclusions.condition: Products of heading 08.01 or 08.02 (coconuts, Brazil nuts, cashew nuts, etc.) or olives
exclusions.action: Redirect to Heading 08.01, 08.02 (Chapter 8) or Chapter 7 or Chapter 20 for olives

exclusions.condition: Residues of headings 23.04 to 23.06 (oil-cake and residues from extraction of vegetable oils)
Seeds of leguminous vegetables or sweet corn even if for sowing (EXCEPT Soya beans 12.01)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 7 (Unless it is Soya beans which stays in 12.01)

exclusions.condition: Spices or other products of Chapter 9 even if for sowing

When considered for Heading 12.09 (Seeds for sowing): Products of headings 12.01 to 12.07 or 12.11 are EXCLUDED from 12.09. They must be classified in their respective headings (12.01-12.07 or 12.11) even if for sowing
exclusions.action: Do NOT classify as 12.09. Redirect to respective heading 12.01-12.07 or 12.11

exclusions.condition: Medicaments (pharmaceutical products) derived from plants of heading 12.11
Perfumery, cosmetic or toilet preparations derived from plants of heading 12.11
Insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, disinfectants or similar products derived from plants of heading 12.11
Dead single-cell micro-organisms (for heading 12.12 seaweeds/algae exclusion)
Cultures of micro-organisms (for heading 12.12 seaweeds/algae exclusion)

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.12 (seeds/grains for specific use) vs Ch.07 (edible vegetables) vs Ch.10 (cereals)
classification_rules.description: Chapter 12 = oil seeds and oleaginous fruits; miscellaneous grains, seeds; industrial/medicinal plants; straw and fodder. Oil seeds (soya, sunflower, rapeseed) even when edible → Ch.12. Edible leguminous vegetables (beans, lentils) → Ch.07. Cereals (wheat, rice) → Ch.10.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: seeds for sowing vs seeds for other purposes
classification_rules.description: Seeds of heading 12.09 = seeds, fruit and spores of a kind used for sowing (certified seed grade). Other seeds of the same species used for food or oil extraction → classified by their primary use heading (Ch.12 oil seeds, Ch.07 vegetables). Seeds for sowing of any plant species fall in 12.09 regardless of the plant's normal chapter.
